validation.spec_summary: The Link Shelf MVP is a minimal Go web application that provides a bookmark service with three core operations: list, create, and delete links. The repository contains a single top‑level directory named 'linkshelf' which holds the Go module definition, server entry point, backend store implementation, HTTP handler definitions, and a tiny static frontend. The data model is defined in internal/store/schema.go, exposing a Link struct with ID, Title, URL, and CreatedAt fields, and an InitSchema function that creates the SQLite table if needed. Store functions reside in internal/store/store.go and operate on a package‑level *sql.DB variable, offering List, Create, and Delete operations with validation for title length and URL scheme. HTTP handlers in internal/api/handlers.go are registered on the default mux and map to REST endpoints under /api/links, while static assets are served from /static/. The server entry point (cmd/server/main.go) opens a SQLite file named linkshelf.db, runs InitSchema, assigns the DB to the store package, registers handlers, and starts listening on port 8080. The frontend under web/ consists of an index.html page with a simple form and a UL for displaying links, app.js which performs AJAX calls to the API for loading, adding, and deleting links, and style.css for basic styling. The definition of done requires that running 'go test ./...' succeeds (even without explicit test files) and that executing 'go run ./cmd/server' launches the UI, allowing a user to add and remove a link. The project is intentionally lightweight, avoiding extra abstraction layers, and is designed to be pipeline‑friendly, with a single verification command that runs all Go tests.
